speaker: FRIAR
text: Did I not tell you she was innocent?

speaker: LEONATO
text: So are the Prince and Claudio, who accused her

speaker: LEONATO
text: Upon the error that you heard debated.

speaker: LEONATO
text: But Margaret was in some fault for this,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Although against her will, as it appears

speaker: LEONATO
text: In the true course of all the question.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Well, I am glad that all things sorts so well.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And so am I, being else by faith enforced

speaker: BENEDICK
text: To call young Claudio to a reckoning for it.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Well, daughter, and you gentlewomen all,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Withdraw into a chamber by yourselves,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And when I send for you, come hither masked.

speaker: LEONATO
text: The Prince and Claudio promised by this hour

speaker: LEONATO
text: To visit me.—You know your office, brother.

speaker: LEONATO
text: You must be father to your brother’s daughter,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And give her to young Claudio.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Which I will do with confirmed countenance.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Friar, I must entreat your pains, I think.

speaker: FRIAR
text: To do what, signior?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: To bind me, or undo me, one of them.—

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Signior Leonato, truth it is, good signior,

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Your niece regards me with an eye of favor.

speaker: LEONATO
text: That eye my daughter lent her; ’tis most true.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And I do with an eye of love requite her.

speaker: LEONATO
text: The sight whereof I think you had from me,

speaker: LEONATO
text: From Claudio, and the Prince. But what’s your will?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Your answer, sir, is enigmatical.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: But for my will, my will is your goodwill

speaker: BENEDICK
text: May stand with ours, this day to be conjoined

speaker: BENEDICK
text: In the state of honorable marriage—

speaker: BENEDICK
text: In which, good friar, I shall desire your help.

speaker: LEONATO
text: My heart is with your liking.

speaker: FRIAR
text: And my help.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Here comes the Prince and Claudio.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Good morrow to this fair assembly.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Good morrow, prince; good morrow, Claudio.

speaker: LEONATO
text: We here attend you. Are you yet determined

speaker: LEONATO
text: Today to marry with my brother’s daughter?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I’ll hold my mind were she an Ethiope.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Call her forth, brother. Here’s the Friar ready.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Good morrow, Benedick. Why, what’s the matter

speaker: PRINCE
text: That you have such a February face,

speaker: PRINCE
text: So full of frost, of storm, and cloudiness?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I think he thinks upon the savage bull.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Tush, fear not, man. We’ll tip thy horns with gold,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And all Europa shall rejoice at thee,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: As once Europa did at lusty Jove

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: When he would play the noble beast in love.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Bull Jove, sir, had an amiable low,

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And some such strange bull leapt your father’s cow

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And got a calf in that same noble feat

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Much like to you, for you have just his bleat.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: For this I owe you. Here comes other reck’nings.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Which is the lady I must seize upon?

speaker: LEONATO
text: This same is she, and I do give you her.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Why, then, she’s mine.—Sweet, let me see your face.

speaker: LEONATO
text: No, that you shall not till you take her hand

speaker: LEONATO
text: Before this friar and swear to marry her.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Give me your hand before this holy friar.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I am your husband, if you like of me.

speaker: HERO
text: And when I lived, I was your other wife,

speaker: HERO
text: And when you loved, you were my other husband.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Another Hero!

speaker: HERO
text: Nothing certainer.

speaker: HERO
text: One Hero died defiled, but I do live,

speaker: HERO
text: And surely as I live, I am a maid.

speaker: PRINCE
text: The former Hero! Hero that is dead!

speaker: LEONATO
text: She died, my lord, but whiles her slander lived.

speaker: FRIAR
text: All this amazement can I qualify,

speaker: FRIAR
text: When after that the holy rites are ended,

speaker: FRIAR
text: I’ll tell you largely of fair Hero’s death.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Meantime let wonder seem familiar,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And to the chapel let us presently.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Soft and fair, friar.—Which is Beatrice?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: I answer to that name. What is your will?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Do not you love me?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Why no, no more than reason.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Why then, your uncle and the Prince and Claudio

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Have been deceived. They swore you did.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Do not you love me?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Troth, no, no more than reason.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Why then, my cousin, Margaret, and Ursula

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Are much deceived, for they did swear you did.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: They swore that you were almost sick for me.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: They swore that you were well-nigh dead for me.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: ’Tis no such matter. Then you do not love me?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: No, truly, but in friendly recompense.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Come, cousin, I am sure you love the gentleman.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And I’ll be sworn upon ’t that he loves her,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: For here’s a paper written in his hand,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: A halting sonnet of his own pure brain,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Fashioned to Beatrice.

speaker: HERO
text: And here’s another,

speaker: HERO
text: Writ in my cousin’s hand, stol’n from her pocket,

speaker: HERO
text: Containing her affection unto Benedick.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: A miracle! Here’s our own hands against

speaker: BENEDICK
text: our hearts. Come, I will have thee, but by this light

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I take thee for pity.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: I would not deny you, but by this good day, I

speaker: BEATRICE
text: yield upon great persuasion, and partly to save your

speaker: BEATRICE
text: life, for I was told you were in a consumption.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Peace! I will stop your mouth.

speaker: PRINCE
text: How dost thou, Benedick, the married man?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I’ll tell thee what, prince: a college of

speaker: BENEDICK
text: wit-crackers cannot flout me out of my humor.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Dost thou think I care for a satire or an epigram?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: No. If a man will be beaten with brains, he shall

speaker: BENEDICK
text: wear nothing handsome about him. In brief, since I

speaker: BENEDICK
text: do purpose to marry, I will think nothing to any

speaker: BENEDICK
text: purpose that the world can say against it, and

speaker: BENEDICK
text: therefore never flout at me for what I have said

speaker: BENEDICK
text: against it. For man is a giddy thing, and this is my

speaker: BENEDICK
text: conclusion.—For thy part, Claudio, I did think to

speaker: BENEDICK
text: have beaten thee, but in that thou art like to be my

speaker: BENEDICK
text: kinsman, live unbruised, and love my cousin.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I had well hoped thou wouldst have denied

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Beatrice, that I might have cudgeled thee out of thy

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: single life, to make thee a double-dealer, which out

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: of question thou wilt be, if my cousin do not look

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: exceeding narrowly to thee.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Come, come, we are friends. Let’s have a

speaker: BENEDICK
text: dance ere we are married, that we may lighten our

speaker: BENEDICK
text: own hearts and our wives’ heels.

speaker: LEONATO
text: We’ll have dancing afterward.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: First, of my word! Therefore play, music.—

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Prince, thou art sad. Get thee a wife, get thee a wife.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: There is no staff more reverend than one tipped

speaker: BENEDICK
text: with horn.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My lord, your brother John is ta’en in flight,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And brought with armed men back to Messina.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Think not on him till tomorrow.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I’ll devise thee brave punishments for him.—Strike

speaker: BENEDICK
text: up, pipers!
